'''Jon David Wilkin''' (born 1 November 1983) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|sr|lf|sh}} or {{rlp|so}} in the 2000s, 2010s and 2020s.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> He played for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]], [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] and the [[Toronto Wolfpack]] at club level and for [[England national rugby league team|England]] and [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] at international level.

==Background==
Wilkin was born in [[Kingston upon Hull]], [[East Yorkshire]], England.

==Club career==
===Hull Kingston Rovers===
Wilkin started his career at [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/2264392.stm|title=Saints swoop for Wilkin
|publisher=BBC|date=17 December 2002|access-date=13 June 2007}}</ref> He made his debut for the club in 2002 which was also his only season at his boyhood club as he signed for Super League club St Helens for [[Super League VIII]] in 2003.

===St Helens===
The 2006 season was a new high point in Wilkin's career. He was a key member of the treble-winning team as Saints won the [[2006 Challenge Cup]], when he played with a broken nose. St Helens reached the [[2006 Super League Grand final]] to be contested against [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]], and Wilkin played at {{rlp|sr}} in Saints' 26–4 victory.

St Helens won the BBC TV Sports Personality of the Year Team of the Year award, with [[Daniel Anderson (rugby league)|Daniel Anderson]] taking the Best Coach honour.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/front_page/6220542.stm|title=Team of the Year – St Helens rugby league side
|publisher=BBC|date=10 December 2006|access-date=13 June 2007}}</ref> Wilkin was recognised for his good season by being named in the 2006 [[Super League Dream Team]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/5357734.stm|title=Saints top Dream Team nominations| publisher=BBC|date=18 September 2006|access-date=13 June 2007}}</ref>

As 2006 Super League champions, St Helens faced 2006 NRL Premiers the [[Brisbane Broncos]] in the [[2007 World Club Challenge]]. Wilkin played at {{rlp|lf}} in the Saints' 18–14 victory.

He played in St Helens' 2008 [[Super League XIII#Grand Final|Super League XIII Grand Final]] defeat by [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]].<ref name="2008 Grand Final">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7642170.stm|title=2008 Grand Final|publisher=BBC|date=4 October 2008|access-date=5 October 2008}}</ref>

He played in the [[2009 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite news|last=AFP|title=Leeds makes it Super League hat-trick|url=http://www.abc.net.au/news/2009-10-11/leeds-makes-it-super-league-hat-trick/1098564|access-date=22 November 2019|newspaper=[[Australian Broadcasting Corporation|ABC]] News|date=11 October 2009}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last=Linfoot|first=Ben|title=Grand Final: Leeds Rhinos 18 St Helens 10|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugbyleague/match_commentary/0,19936,11070_3204177,00.html|access-date=22 November 2019|newspaper=Sky Sports|date=10 October 2009|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100213070441/http://www.skysports.com/rugbyleague/match_commentary/0,19936,11070_3204177,00.html|archive-date=13 February 2010}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last=Fletcher|first=Paul|title=St Helens 10-18 Leeds Rhinos|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/8290503.stm|access-date=22 November 2019|newspaper=BBC Sport|date=10 October 2009}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last=Stewart|first=Rob|title=Lee Smith targets place in England rugby union team after Grand Final victory|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/6299292/Lee-Smith-targets-place-in-England-rugby-union-team-after-Grand-Final-victory.html|access-date=22 November 2019|newspaper=The Telegraph|date=12 October 2009}}</ref>

He played in the [[2011 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2011/oct/08/super-league-grand-final-match-report?newsfeed=true|title=Leeds claim Grand Final glory as inspired Rob Burrow sinks St Helens|date=8 October 2011|work=Guardian|access-date=21 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/8815944/St-Helens-16-Leeds-32-match-report.html|title=St Helens 16 Leeds 32|date=8 October 2011|work=Daily Telegraph|access-date=21 November 2019}}</ref>

===Toronto Wolfpack===
Wilkin signed for the [[Toronto Wolfpack]] ahead of the 2019 season. In July 2020 Wilkin announced that he would retire at the end of the 2020 season.<ref>{{cite web|publisher=St Helens Reporter|date=29 Jul 2020|access-date=26 Dec 2020|title=Former Saints' skipper Jon Wilkin is to bring down the curtain on his rugby league career following Toronto Wolfpack's demise|url=https://www.sthelensreporter.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/i-will-not-play-professional-rugby-again-unless-miracle-happens-says-jon-wilkin-2927195}}</ref>

==International career==
[[File:Jon Wilkin.jpg|right|thumb|Wilkin playing against the Kiwis at the 2008 RLWC]]
Wilkin was called in to the Great Britain squad [[2005 Rugby League Tri-Nations|2005 Tri Nations]] but did not feature in the competition.

He was included in the 25-man Great Britain squad for the [[2006 Rugby League Tri-Nations|2006 Tri-Nations]] tour of Australia and New Zealand. Wilkin made his international début from the bench at the [[Sydney Football Stadium (1988)|Sydney Football Stadium]], Sydney on 4 November 2006 against Australia. He came off the bench in two further games in that series, the first against New Zealand in [[Wellington]], and the second against Australia in [[Brisbane]].

Wilkin was selected for the [[England national rugby league team|England]] squad to compete in the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]] tournament in Australia.<ref name="Purdham earns World Cup call-up">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7656553.stm|title=Purdham earns World Cup call-up|publisher=BBC|date=7 October 2008|access-date=7 October 2008}}</ref> In the first match against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] he played from the interchange bench in England's victory.

==Personal life==
Wilkin won [[The Observer]]'s first Community Champion Award.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.saintsrlfc.com/news/336|title=Wilks wins Observer Community Award|publisher=Saints|date=12 June 2007|access-date=13 June 2007}}</ref>

In January 2012, it was announced that Wilkin was to be the first chairman of the newly founded Players' Association; League 13.<ref>{{cite news|last=BBC Sport|title=Rugby League players form their own union|work=BBC Sport |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/16691672|access-date=7 June 2012}}</ref>

In 2015 Wilkin opened an independent coffee shop in partnership with former St Helens teammate [[Mark Flanagan (rugby league)|Mark Flanagan]] in Manchester city centre.<ref>{{cite web|last1=O'Leary|first1=Abigail|title=Top rugby league players turn their hands to making artisan coffee in new business quest|url=http://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/news/greater-manchester-news/top-rugby-league-players-turn-9060907|website=Manchester Evening News|access-date=12 December 2017|date=17 April 2015}}</ref>

In 2013, Wilkin married his first wife Megan although they subsequently divorced<ref>{{Cite web |title=Megan & Jon Wilkin’s Wedding Whitewell & Stoneyhurst |url=https://www.weddingsbyluke.co.uk/2014/01/megan-jon-wilkins-wedding-whitewell-stoneyhurst/ |access-date=2026-05-03 |language=en-GB}}</ref> <ref>{{Cite web |date=2015-02-06 |title=Jon Wilkin: Saints now better at getting most out of 'young man's squad |url=https://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/sport/11776444.jon-wilkin-saints-now-better-at-getting-most-out-of-young-mans-squad/ |access-date=2026-05-03 |website=St Helens Star |language=en}}</ref>. In 2018, Wilkin married British swimmer [[Fran Halsall]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2020/01/29/super-league-ready-kick-off-on-field-eyes-big-spending-toronto/ |title=Super League ready for kick-off with all on-field eyes on big-spending Toronto Wolfpack |last=Heppenstall |first=Ross |date=29 January 2020 |access-date=26 August 2025 |website=The Telegraph |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230108215551/https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2020/01/29/super-league-ready-kick-off-on-field-eyes-big-spending-toronto/ |archive-date=8 January 2023}}</ref> In 2022, Wilkin joined Sky Sports to become one of the pundits for their rugby league coverage.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/13312676/salford-red-devils-jon-wilkin-slams-super-league-club-for-embarrassing-game-with-irresponsible-squad-selection/|title=Salford Red Devils: Jon Wilkin slams Super League club for 'embarrassing' game with 'irresponsible' squad selection|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>
